concept: Would you design a turn-based game where units on the battlefield are connected by energy tethers that provide buffs when properly aligned but vulnerabilities when disrupted? Strategic positioning revolves around creating geometric patterns with these tethers to maximize synchronized unit effectiveness. Enemy forces specifically target these connections, requiring constant adaptation and reformation of tether networks. The environment contains conduit points that can redirect, strengthen, or specialize tether effects when incorporated into a formation. Your forces are attempting to reestablish an ancient energy grid by connecting sacred sites with these tactical formations, each successful battle permanently restoring power to different regions.
